title: ALBIGLUTIDE
description: Albiglutide is a once-weekly glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist used for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus. It is a long-acting analog with a half-life of approximately 5 days, created by fusing GLP-1 to human albumin, which provides extended duration of action. Albiglutide works by stimulating insulin secretion in response to elevated blood glucose, suppressing glucagon release, slowing gastric emptying, and promoting satiety.

research.title: Albiglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease (Harmony Outcomes): a double-blind, randomised placebo-controlled trial
research.summary: The Harmony Outcomes trial demonstrated that albiglutide significantly reduced major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with type 2 diabetes and established cardiovascular disease compared to placebo.

research.title: Cardiovascular, mortality, and kidney outcomes with GLP-1 receptor agonists in patients with type 2 diabetes: a systematic review and meta-analysis of cardiovascular outcome trials
research.summary: Meta-analysis of cardiovascular outcome trials showed GLP-1 receptor agonists, including albiglutide, provide significant benefits for cardiovascular events, mortality, and kidney outcomes in type 2 diabetes patients.

research.title: Effects of albiglutide on myocardial infarction and ischaemic heart disease outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease in the Harmony Outcomes trial
research.summary: Albiglutide demonstrated significant reductions in myocardial infarction and ischemic heart disease outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and established cardiovascular disease.

research.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists in the treatment of type 2 diabetes - state-of-the-art
research.summary: Comprehensive review showing albiglutide is administered once weekly, shares common mechanisms with other GLP-1 RAs including augmentation of insulin secretion, suppression of glucagon, and delayed gastric emptying.

research.title: The safety of albiglutide for the treatment of type 2 diabetes
research.summary: Albiglutide has significantly fewer gastrointestinal side effects compared to other GLP-1 receptor agonists and appears safe across all stages of renal failure, though concerns exist about pancreatitis cases and injection site reactions.

research.title: Postprandial plasma glucose effects of once-weekly albiglutide for the treatment of type 2 diabetes
research.summary: Albiglutide consistently lowered postprandial glucose levels after each meal in addition to reducing fasting plasma glucose at clinically relevant doses of 30-64 mg.

research.title: Effects of GLP-1 and Its Analogs on Gastric Physiology in Diabetes Mellitus and Obesity
research.summary: GLP-1 analogs including albiglutide slow gastric emptying and increase gastric volumes, which contributes to reduced postprandial glycemia and promotes satiety.

research.title: GLP-1 receptor agonists for individualized treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus
research.summary: Long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonists like albiglutide provide continuous receptor activation and primarily lower fasting glucose through sustained insulin secretion, while short-acting agents mainly affect postprandial glucose.

sentiment.summary: Limited user experiences available for albiglutide. As a prescription GLP-1 receptor agonist primarily used for diabetes management, community discussions are sparse compared to more accessible supplements.

effects.label: Cardiovascular Outcomes:
effects.description: Demonstrates significant reductions in cardiovascular events in high-risk patients with type 2 diabetes

effects.label: Glucose Reduction:
effects.description: Effectively lowers both fasting and postprandial plasma glucose at doses of 30-64 mg weekly

effects.label: Comparative Efficacy:
effects.description: Shows slightly less HbA1c reduction and weight loss compared to some competitor GLP-1 agonists like liraglutide

effects.label: Renal Safety:
effects.description: Proven safe and effective across all stages of renal failure without dose adjustment requirements

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: Once-weekly subcutaneous injection of 30-50 mg as maintenance therapy

dosage.label: Duration of Action:
dosage.description: Half-life of approximately 5 days allows for weekly administration

dosage.label: Clinical Range:
dosage.description: Studied doses range from 30-64 mg weekly in phase 2 and 3 trials

dosage.label: Administration:
dosage.description: Administered via subcutaneous injection using pre-filled pen device

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Tolerance:
sideEffects.description: Significantly fewer GI side effects (nausea, vomiting) compared to other GLP-1 receptor agonists, with rates similar to placebo

sideEffects.label: Injection Site Reactions:
sideEffects.description: Reported in up to 2% of patients, occasionally leading to treatment discontinuation

sideEffects.label: Pancreatitis Concern:
sideEffects.description: Imbalance of pancreatitis cases noted in approval program, though overall incidence remains low

sideEffects.label: Hypoglycemia Risk:
sideEffects.description: Low risk when used as monotherapy, but increased risk when combined with sulfonylureas or insulin

availability.label: Prescription Status:
availability.description: Available by prescription for type 2 diabetes management in patients requiring injectable therapy

availability.label: Market Status:
availability.description: Originally marketed but subsequently discontinued by manufacturer in 2017 despite proven efficacy and safety profile

availability.label: Clinical Use:
availability.description: Primarily studied and used in patients with type 2 diabetes who have not achieved glycemic control with oral medications
